{{Quote|Ah. It is a rare antiquity from the days of the Old Republic.|Danta Belissa|<ref name="GG3-1" />}}
'''Danta Belissa''' was a [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and souvenir [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] who ran a small [[Danta Belissa's store|souvenir and antique shop]] in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] by around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. In that [[Time/Legends|time]], Belissa's store ran into trouble when the criminal [[Wonn Ionstrike/Legends|Wonn Ionstrike]] sent thugs to Belissa's store for protection [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]]. The merchant's problems were only compounded when he reported the crimes to the [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Bespin Wing Guard]] and one of their [[Officer/Legends|officers]], [[Jerrol Blendin]], decided to further extort Belissa for [[Currency/Legends|money]].

When Belissa subsequently began clearing his shop, he ended up discovering an exposed [[Gas/Legends|gas]] conduit that piped harvested [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] to Cloud City's other levels. Seeing an opportunity to financially help his family and business, Belissa attached a siphon to the pipe and started discretely selling the pipe's tibanna gas to [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and rogues. He kept his bootlegging business a secret to both Ionstrike and Blendin, although Belissa's fate during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] [[occupation of Bespin]] was unknown.

==Biography==
===Initial business and troubles===
{{Quote|It may be hard to find, but it's always worth the trip.|Danta Belissa's store motto|<ref name="GG3-2">[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]]</ref>}}
[[File:JerrolBlendin-TPOV.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Jerrol Blendin, a Wing Guard officer who proved no different than the criminal Wonn Ionstrike in extorting Belissa]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Danta Belissa<ref name="GG3-1" /> was a [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and souvenir [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] who had a [[Danta Belissa's wife|wife]] and [[Danta Belissa's daughter|daughter]] prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] fought between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="GG3-2" /> By around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="1 ABY">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[occupation of Bespin]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Because [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] describes [[Wonn Ionstrike/Legends|Wonn Ionstrike]] first causing trouble for Danta Belissa's store about two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] occupation, the troubles must have taken place around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].</ref> he<ref name="GG3-1" /> ran a small [[Danta Belissa's store|souvenir and antique shop]] on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. The store was located on level 120 of Cloud City's [[Port Town (Cloud City)/Legends|Port Town]], and sold rich, [[Spice/Legends|spicy]] [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] food for the level's administrative personnel. Much of the cooking was done by the shopkeeper's wife and daughter in an adjacent restaurant while Belissa himself ran the shop's business, managing five full-time employees.<ref name="GG3-2" /> At one point, Belissa spoke about something he referred to as a rare antiquity from the [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].<ref name="GG3-1" />

Around 1 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="1 ABY" /> a criminal operating in Port Town named [[Wonn Ionstrike/Legends|Wonn Ionstrike]] sent thugs to Belissa's store for protection [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]]. The shopkeeper reported the crimes to the [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Bespin Wing Guard]], although one of its [[Officer/Legends|officers]], [[Jerrol Blendin]], was no more honorable than the criminals, and individually extorted the merchant as well. Belissa's [[Currency/Legends|money]] thus was given to both Ionstrike and Blendin.<ref name="GG3-2" />

===Bootleg tibanna gas sales and occupation of Bespin===
[[File:Ionstrike.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Wonn Ionstrike, who remained unaware of Belissa's discrete siphon sales in response to the criminal's extortion]]
Belissa initially began to pack up his shop, discovering an exposed [[Gas/Legends|gas]] conduit that piped harvested [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] to Cloud City's other levels when he moved one of his storage room's fryer units. Placing a small siphon into the pipe, Belissa sold bootleg tibanna gas to [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and rogues, and was able to make enough money from selling the gas to keep his store in business.<ref name="GG3-2" /> By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the shopkeeper was still able to keep his tibanna bootlegging secret from Ionstrike and Blendin.<ref name="GG3-2" />

In 3 ABY, the Empire [[Occupation of Bespin|occupied]] Bespin with the intent to capture the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Following the occupation,<ref name="TNEC" /> the planet's [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], announced for the city's occupants to evacuate. Belissa's subsequent fate was unknown.<ref name="GG3-2" />

==Personality and traits==
Danta Belissa stood 1.65 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="GG3-1" />

==Skills and abilities==
Belissa had knowledge of business<ref name="GG3-2" /> and [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history. He also had bargaining skills<ref name="GG3-1" /> and had both the intuition to financially help his family and business through seeing an opportunity to discretely siphon tibanna gas for profit and the ability to keep that bootlegging a secret from Ionstrike and Blendin.<ref name="GG3-2" /> He was capable of operating a [[Cloud car/Legends|cloud car]]<ref name="GG3-1" /> and its [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]].<ref name="GG3-2" />

==Equipment==
Belissa's equipment included a [[credit changer]]. He also wore a [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] and [[Boot/Legends|boots]].<ref name="GG3-1" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Danta Belissa was created for the [[1989]] first edition of the [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' as an example of a standard Cloud City citizen, where he was given roleplaying statistics.<ref name="GG3-1" />
